name: Ashwagandha
use: Rasayana (rejuvenative tonic); prescribed for debility, emaciation, and chronic stress management.

mechanisms.title: HPA Axis Modulation
mechanisms.desc: Reduces cortisol secretion by modulating the hypothalamic–pituitary–adrenal axis.

mechanisms.title: GABAergic Activity
mechanisms.desc: Promotes sleep onset and anxiety mitigation via GABA receptor potentiation.

application: Decoction: 3-6g of root powder boiled in milk, taken twice daily.

name: Turmeric
use: Widely used for wound healing, systemic skin disorders, and purifying hepatobiliary pathways.

mechanisms.title: Pleiotropic Anti-Inflammation
mechanisms.desc: Simultaneously downregulates COX-2, LOX, iNOS, and NF-κB transcriptive pathways.

mechanisms.title: Antioxidant Cascade
mechanisms.desc: Activates Nrf2 transcription factors, upregulating endogenous antioxidant profiles.

application: Infusion: 1-2g of expressed rhizome juice or powder mixed with warm water or clarified butter.

name: Brahmi
use: Classified as a premium Medhya Rasayana to enhance cognitive memory, focus, and neurological processing.

mechanisms.title: Synaptic Remodelling
mechanisms.desc: Bacosides enhance synaptic plasticity by promoting dendritic arborisation within the hippocampus.

mechanisms.title: AChE Inhibition
mechanisms.desc: Inhibits acetylcholinesterase enzyme profiles, raising active acetylcholine availability.

application: Juice: 10-20ml of fresh expressed whole plant extract, consumed in the morning.

name: Neem
use: Core systemic approach for infectious skin disorders, metabolic heat conditions, and pathogen removal.

mechanisms.title: Antimicrobial Action
mechanisms.desc: Disrupts bacterial peptidoglycan wall architectures and halts biofilm clustering safely.

mechanisms.title: Immunomodulation
mechanisms.desc: Nimbolide structures directly activate tissue macrophage populations for clearance.

application: Paste: Fresh leaves ground with water applied topically; Paste or Decoction (5-10g) internally.

name: Amalaki
use: A primary cornerstone for structural longevity (Vayasthapana); highly regarded for eye health and metabolic balance.

mechanisms.title: Mitochondrial Protection
mechanisms.desc: Acts as a powerful antioxidant pathway donor, neutralizing toxic intracellular free radical accumulations.

mechanisms.title: Collagen Synthesis Stabilization
mechanisms.desc: Upregulates regular transcriptive collagen production, preserving structural extracellular matrix profiles.

application: Churna: 3-6g of dried fruit powder administered with warm water or organic honey daily.

name: Arjuna
use: The signature cardiotonic (Hridaya) used to fortify myocardial tissues and regulate systemic blood patterns.

mechanisms.title: Myocardial Inotropy
mechanisms.desc: Enhances systemic intracellular calcium turnover, boosting cardiac contraction efficiency smoothly.

mechanisms.title: Endothelial Integrity Enhancement
mechanisms.desc: Suppresses localized vascular lipid peroxidation and strengthens delicate coronary artery lining.

application: Ksheerapaka: 3-6g of bark powder decocted in a mixture of 4 parts milk and 16 parts water until only milk remains.

name: Tulasi
use: Prescribed widely for acute upper respiratory conditions, running fevers, and environmental stress profiling.

mechanisms.title: NK Cell Activation
mechanisms.desc: Enhances host immunomodulatory responses by maximizing target Natural Killer (NK) and T-helper cellular activity.

mechanisms.title: COX-2 Inhibition
mechanisms.desc: Eugenol molecules selectively inhibit cyclooxygenase-2 profiles to safely lower localized respiratory irritation.

application: Svarasa: 5-10ml of freshly expressed leaf juice taken along with raw honey twice everyday.

name: Yashtimadhu
use: An exceptional demulcent agent utilized to coat inflamed throats, heal peptic ulcers, and balance voice quality.

mechanisms.title: Mucosal Shielding Cascade
mechanisms.desc: Stimulates localized mucus secretion across visceral tracks, providing a protective structural lining coating.

mechanisms.title: Glucocorticoid Potentiation
mechanisms.desc: Inhibits 11-beta-HSD, extending the presence of native cortisol to reduce inflammatory profiles naturally.

application: Churna: 1-3g of fine root powder mixed thoroughly into warm water or clarified butter after meals.

name: Pipali
use: A powerful bio-enhancing catalyst (Yogavahi) used to treat sluggish digestion, asthma, and chronic metabolic drops.

mechanisms.title: Bioavailability Enhancement
mechanisms.desc: Inhibits hepatic glucuronidation, significantly increasing the systemic absorption of other co-administered herb profiles.

mechanisms.title: Thermogenic Induction
mechanisms.desc: Stimulates specific local enzyme profiles inside the stomach lining, increasing baseline metabolic fire cleanly.

application: Vardhamana Rasayana: A systematic daily step-up dosage schedule starting from 1 spike up to 10 spikes, boiled in milk.

name: Kumari
use: Prescribed to regulate irregular menstrual flows, cool systemic hepatic heat, and relieve severe digestive stagnation.

mechanisms.title: Peristaltic Stimulation
mechanisms.desc: Aloin structures irritate the inner bowel lining cleanly, drawing water into the tract to initiate smooth elimination cycles.

mechanisms.title: Fibroblast Proliferation
mechanisms.desc: Acemannan structures directly prompt cellular skin repair networks to accelerate tissue healing scores.

application: Gel: 15-30ml of fresh inner leaf mucilage consumed on an empty stomach first thing in the morning.

name: Guggulu
use: The premier choice for removing metabolic accumulation (Ama), clearing arterial pathways, and relieving joint discomfort.

mechanisms.title: FXR Antagonism
mechanisms.desc: Guggulsterones act as highly competitive antagonists at the Farnesoid X Receptor, effectively accelerating lipid turnover.

mechanisms.title: Chondrocyte Protection
mechanisms.desc: Suppresses localized matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs), protecting articular cartilage layouts from degrade stresses.

application: Vati (Tablet): 1-2g of carefully purified resin formulation administered with warm water or ginger tea.

name: Punarnava
use: Literally translating to 'the duplicator of youth'; standard selection for systemic water retention and renal health.

mechanisms.title: Renal Vasodilation
mechanisms.desc: Punarnavine compounds step up internal glomerular filtration rates, initiating clean, fluid-balancing diuretic pathways.

mechanisms.title: Hepatoprotective Stabilization
mechanisms.desc: Maintains normal serum alanine aminotransferase (ALT) markers against baseline chemical stressors.

application: Decoction: 20-30ml of the freshly prepared root extraction consumed twice over the course of a day.

name: Vacha
use: A traditional brain-stimulating option (Samjna-sthapana) relied on to clear vocal channels and enhance speech clarity.

mechanisms.title: Neuroprotective Channel Gating
mechanisms.desc: Asarone variations interact directly with specific NMDA receptor tracking metrics, preventing structural cell burnout.

mechanisms.title: Bronchodilator Action
mechanisms.desc: Relaxes smooth vascular ring layouts inside breathing networks, opening pathways smoothly.

application: Churna: A highly limited micro-dosage (125-500mg) administered along with authentic organic honey.

name: Bhumyamalaki
use: The absolute primary choice for clearing jaundice patterns, balancing fatty liver states, and blocking cellular viral copies.

mechanisms.title: Polymerase Blockade
mechanisms.desc: Extracts actively inhibit endogenous viral DNA polymerase enzymes, safely halting target replication chains.

mechanisms.title: Glycogen Storage Restoration
mechanisms.desc: Protects and restores intracellular glycogen stores inside overstressed liver tissue sheets cleanly.

application: Juice or Paste: 10-20ml fresh juice or 5g fresh paste consumed before midday meals regularly.
